description: Repo-owned system harness for active Wiii product contracts. It validates that critical architecture scenarios remain explicit and tied to living runtime, test, docs, and CI evidence.

scenarios.title: System flow observability map stays current
scenarios.contract: Wiii system control plane maps layers, runtime flows, monitoring signals, and next execution order before runtime fixes begin.
invariants: The control-plane map names Wiii layers, active runtime flows, and first debugging checkpoints.
The flow-monitoring ladder distinguishes existing evidence from gaps to close.

Stream and Wiii Connect action correlation preserve middleware/header/tool-state request_id or generate a fallback before ledger, lifecycle, heartbeat metadata, logs, finalization continuity, action audit output, provider-call telemetry, aggregate doctor metrics, Prometheus-compatible runtime alert counters, and deployable runtime-flow/finalization/memory-maintenance alert rules are emitted.
The map states that production smoke does not prove deep LMS preview/apply acceptance.
The map identifies ledger-backed replay as the next acceptance target after the runtime flow ledger.
The OpenClaw reference audit records control-plane patterns, non-copy boundaries, and chat-baseline requirements.
The Self-Harness workflow re-runs when the control-plane document changes.

verification.purpose: Prove the system control-plane map remains present and tied to the Self-Harness contract.

scenarios.title: System comprehension reference stays guarded
scenarios.contract: Understand-Anything is adopted as a local-only system-comprehension reference with generated graph output ignored and scan guardrails tracked.
invariants: Understand-Anything remains a supporting comprehension reference, not a runtime dependency or test replacement.
The focused audit records the inspected commit, bounded Wiii trial, dependency hubs, and adoption decision.
The repo-owned wrapper exposes scoped flow profiles for chat, LMS document preview, visual/Code Studio, and harness governance maps.
The wrapper writes generated scan, import-map, and hotspot summary output only under ignored `.understand-anything/tmp/`.
Generated `.understand-anything/` graph and intermediate output stay ignored.
A tracked `.understandignore` excludes generated output, local agent scratch, logs, secrets, binary assets, and build artifacts.
The Self-Harness workflow re-runs when the comprehension audit, wrapper, or scan guardrail changes.

must_contain: navigation aid, not proof of runtime behavior

verification.purpose: Prove the system-comprehension reference remains documented, ignored, and tied to workflow evidence.
Prove the scoped Understand-Anything wrapper selects Wiii flow files and emits guarded summaries without requiring the reference clone in unit tests.

scenarios.title: Memory and context provenance stays inspectable
scenarios.contract: RuntimeFlowLedger embeds a privacy-safe Context Provenance Ledger v1 for conversation, document, memory, and host context sources.
invariants: Context provenance is emitted as counts, bounded names, hashes, and warning codes, never raw prompt, memory, or uploaded document text.
Uploaded document context records attachment counts, parser names, media kinds, source-ref counts, and provenance warnings.
Memory context records typed memory counts when present and warns when only untyped semantic_context text is available.
Memory-turn acceptance requires ready semantic retrieval plus relevant memory evidence in the terminal runtime ledger.
Browser memory acceptance renders the user-facing MemoryTab summary from the API contract and proves clear-all updates the aggregate strip without exposing provenance internals.
Visual and Code Studio backend replay requires observed tool names plus stream lifecycle event counts in the terminal runtime ledger.
Normal chat no-action replay forbids Pointy, host-action, visual, and Code Studio stream events in the terminal runtime ledger.
Episodic prior-session recall records typed counts, event types, score range, and org-scope flags without raw recalled snippets.
Host context records declared surface and capabilities without granting new action permission.
The OpenHuman reference audit documents adopt and do-not-copy boundaries.

Prove the OpenHuman-derived provenance contract remains tied to runtime, test, and docs evidence.

scenarios.title: Wiii Connect tool event boundary stays public-safe
scenarios.contract: Direct tool calls, tool results, host-action params, and runtime traces redact provider/account controls before entering public event ledgers.
invariants: Tool call args are sanitized before SSE publication and before appending to tool_call_events.
Tool result ledgers are sanitized before appending to tool_call_events while internal direct and Code Studio tool messages keep raw results for synthesis, visual emission, and artifact delivery.
Wiii Connect/provider result JSON preserves public action status and summary fields but removes connection_ref, connection IDs, Page IDs, provider payloads, approval tokens, and secret-like text.
Host-action params sent from direct visual events remove backend-owned provider controls before publication.
Policy-denied calls and forced shortcuts use the same public-safe event boundary.

must_contain: Direct tool dispatch events are part of the same public surface

verification.command: cd maritime-ai-service; python -m pytest tests/unit/test_direct_tool_post_dispatch_runtime.py tests/unit/test_direct_tool_rounds_runtime.py tests/unit/test_code_studio_fast_paths.py tests/unit/test_stream_tool_events.py tests/unit/test_stream_event_public_boundary.py tests/unit/test_direct_tool_dispatch_runtime.py tests/unit/test_wiii_runtime_flow_acceptance.py -q --tb=short
verification.purpose: Prove public tool-call args, public tool-result payloads, tool-result ledgers, host-action params, Code Studio ledgers, stream factories, and graph forwarding redact provider/account controls without removing internal raw tool messages.
Prove the Wiii Connect public event boundary remains tied to docs, runtime, and regression evidence.

scenarios.title: Ordinary chat baseline stays on the safe chat lane
scenarios.contract: Normal Vietnamese chat streams must complete through the non-tool chat lane with runtime-flow evidence, safe tool suppression, durable finalization, and no raw host/action payloads in the answer.
invariants: Baseline chat records host_surface and native chat route evidence.
Host actions, Pointy, visual runtime, and Code Studio are suppressed when the user did not request them.
The terminal done event carries runtime_flow_ledger with done_seen=true.
The final answer is persisted through finalize_response_turn and does not expose raw provider, host-action, widget, or tool-call JSON.
No uploaded-document prompt asks for missing context instead of inventing document content.
Slow toolless provider streams emit heartbeat/status evidence and still finalize.

scenarios.title: Visual intent binds the correct tool capability
scenarios.contract: VisualIntentDecision -> VisualToolRequirement -> direct and Code Studio tool collection
invariants: required_visual_tool_names is the single source of required visual tool names.
Chart and article figure requests keep tool_generate_visual.
Code Studio app and artifact requests keep tool_create_visual_code.
Mermaid requests stay on the Mermaid lane and do not drift to chart or app tools.
Explicit web search remains stronger than visual intent and may strip visual tools.

scenarios.title: Code Studio scaffold fallback is policy-gated
scenarios.contract: Provider/tool-loop failures resolve to typed CodeStudioToolRoundOutcome values before any scaffold is injected.
invariants: The tool loop records why a fallback boundary was reached.
Generic simulation or app requests stop safely instead of receiving a broad template.
Only artifact contracts can engage the deterministic scaffold fallback.
Synthetic scaffold tool calls always target tool_create_visual_code.

scenarios.title: Uploaded LMS documents use preview/apply approval
scenarios.contract: Uploaded document authoring must emit a preview host action with source references before any LMS apply flow uses an approval token.
invariants: Uploaded document authoring binds preview host actions, not direct LMS mutation.
Lesson and course preview tool names are shared by one contract module.
Preview payloads must carry source references or citations from the uploaded document.
Apply is outside Wiii runtime mutation and requires host approval_token or preview token handling.
Frontend apply forwards approval_token only to the host apply request and keeps it out of Wiii audit payloads.
Browser acceptance exercises embed LMS preview apply through a parent iframe host bridge and keeps approval_token out of Wiii audit payloads.
Legacy direct course mutation is guarded by an explicit safe contract.

scenarios.title: Host action audit route remains available and token-safe

invariants: The /api/v1/host-actions/audit route remains registered.
Audit writes include authenticated user and organization context.
Preview tokens are hashed and never persisted in clear text.
Host action audit tests exercise route submission and token hashing.

scenarios.title: Frontend visual and Code Studio shells avoid raw output drift
scenarios.contract: VisualBlock and CodeStudioPanel keep app previews in host-owned frames, preserve mobile-safe layout, and avoid rendering raw code when preview is selected.
invariants: Completed Code Studio sessions prefer preview unless code view was explicitly requested.
Code Studio previews render as immersive app frames with viewport sizing.
The overlay shell remains mobile-safe before the small breakpoint.
VisualBlock delegates an open Code Studio session instead of duplicating the frame.
The Runtime tab renders sanitized runtime_flow_ledger facts and warns on missing visual/Code Studio lifecycle or no-action event leakage.
The Runtime ledger browser acceptance opens Wiii Connect Runtime from a seeded authenticated desktop session and proves token-like ledger values are redacted.
Terminal done runtime_flow_ledger payloads from visual and Code Studio chat streams survive SSE parsing and reach the Runtime tab.

Source-backed document, semantic memory, and episodic prior-session context provenance is visible in the Runtime tab as source kinds, memory signals, episodic counts/event types/score range/scope flags, warning codes, and hash/count-only privacy metadata.

Subagent parent/child boundary evidence is visible in the Runtime tab as report counts, projected/dropped-key counts, source/tool counts, thinking-dropped counts, and warning codes without child working memory.
Backend runtime acceptance browser_replay evidence can seed the Runtime tab without raw prompt or answer leakage.

Authenticated embed LMS browser acceptance uploads course content through the paperclip flow, carries host/document context in the stream request, persists an lms_document_preview terminal ledger with preview-required, approval-evidence-present, no-apply-attempted flags and without raw document text, and exercises preview apply through a parent iframe host bridge.
Vietnamese-first UI copy remains covered by focused tests.
